sessions.summary: Deep research into TS2322 fingerprint-only divergence patterns; no code changes committed this session
sessions.invariant: Fingerprint-only failures fall into distinct, separable patterns; each needs its own targeted fix rather than one broad change
sessions.kpi_impact: Research-only: documented concrete divergence patterns across 20+ representative TS2322 fingerprint-only tests to help future iterations batch-target patterns

promising_leads.pattern: Alias preservation for unions from generic type aliases
promising_leads.example: iterableTReturnTNext.ts: tsc shows 'IteratorResult<number, undefined>' (preserves alias); tsz expands to 'IteratorYieldResult<number> | IteratorReturnResult<undefined>'
promising_leads.estimated_impact: Multiple tests - unknown but potentially high
promising_leads.file_pointer: crates/tsz-solver/src/diagnostics/format/mod.rs:460-491 — `is_simple_type` currently includes TypeData::Union, which skips display_alias redirect. Policy: should distinguish simple-literal unions (expand) from alias-backed unions with Application origin (preserve)
promising_leads.risk: Medium - broad change; may regress tests where literal unions should be expanded (keyof, ValueOf<Obj>). Needs careful gate — only redirect when alias_origin is an Application and args are not all literals

promising_leads.pattern: Awaited<T> not simplified in error messages when T is non-promise
promising_leads.example: inferFromGenericFunctionReturnTypes3.ts: tsz shows 'Promise<Awaited<boolean>>', tsc shows the reduced form
promising_leads.estimated_impact: Small to medium (tests involving Promise inference)
promising_leads.file_pointer: crates/tsz-solver/src/evaluation/evaluate_rules/conditional.rs and generic_call/return_context.rs already have Awaited-aware code — display/formatter should also evaluate Application<Awaited, [T]> before printing when the result is identity
promising_leads.risk: Low to medium - need to ensure no infinite loops in evaluation during display

promising_leads.pattern: typeof ClassName should resolve to *Constructor alias (e.g., DateConstructor)
promising_leads.example: didYouMeanElaborationsForExpressionsWhichCouldBeCalled.ts: tsz shows 'Type Date missing props', tsc shows 'Type DateConstructor missing props' — the constructor interface alias
promising_leads.estimated_impact: Small (tests involving class value assignment to instance type)
promising_leads.file_pointer: crates/tsz-solver/src/diagnostics/format/mod.rs - when formatting a constructor type that originated from `declare var X: XConstructor`, check if the referenced constructor type has a named interface alias and use it

promising_leads.pattern: Union element ordering for intersected numeric literals
promising_leads.example: inDoesNotOperateOnPrimitiveTypes.ts: tsc shows 'T & (0 | 2 | 1)', tsz shows 'T & (0 | 1 | 2)' (ascending sort)
promising_leads.file_pointer: Union canonicalization - tsz preserves insertion order; tsc uses a specific order that differs from ascending sort. Need to match tsc's intersection distribution order logic
promising_leads.risk: Medium - may regress tests that expect alphabetical order

promising_leads.pattern: Spurious '_default' in namespace imports with export default
promising_leads.example: exportAssignmentExpressionIsExpressionNode.ts: tsz shows '{ _default: ..., default: ..., configs: ... }' (includes internal const name); tsc shows only '{ default: ..., configs: ... }'
promising_leads.file_pointer: Module/namespace export handling - when collecting members for namespace object, internal variable names (like `_default`) should not be exposed alongside their export alias (`default`)
promising_leads.risk: Medium - module resolution is a sensitive area

promising_leads.pattern: Target type display for excess property check on unions
promising_leads.example: excessPropertyCheckWithUnions.ts: tsc shows full union '{ tag: A; x: string } | { tag: A; y: number }' as target; tsz narrows to single branch '{ tag: A; x: string }'
promising_leads.file_pointer: crates/tsz-checker/src/error_reporter (or solver diagnostic builders) — when reporting TS2353 for excess property against union target, should display the full union rather than the matched branch

promising_leads.pattern: Narrowing via type predicates on array filter
promising_leads.example: inferTypePredicates.ts: Multiple false positives like 'Type (number | null)[] is not assignable to type number[]' after .filter(isDefined) call
promising_leads.estimated_impact: High - dozens of tests use this pattern
promising_leads.file_pointer: Narrowing in checker + inference of type predicate functions (user-defined type guards passed to Array.filter)
promising_leads.risk: High - narrowing changes have broad ripple effects

promising_leads.pattern: Fresh literal assignability against keyof T
promising_leads.example: keyofIsLiteralContexualType.ts: tsz emits TS2322 for 'a' against keyof T; tsc allows because keyof T constraint is {a:string,b:string}
promising_leads.file_pointer: Assignability from string literal to keyof T with constraint narrowing to literal union

cross_cutting_blockers: Many divergences combine a display issue with a semantic narrowing issue — fixing display alone won't flip tests that also have narrowing bugs
Fingerprint count mismatches (e.g., tsz emits 8 TS2416 on MyMap extends Map vs tsc 0) often indicate over-emission from missing method/property variance checks
Position off-by-few-columns (e.g., line 13 vs 20 in conditionalTypeDoesntSpinForever) suggests diagnostic anchor mismatches in conditional type returns

sampled_tests_detail.description: Concrete diffs gathered 2026-04-16 from running fingerprint-only tests with --print-fingerprints. Use these to validate that a proposed fix actually addresses the root cause.

tests.arrayFrom.ts: Type 'A[]' vs 'unknown[]' — inference fallback to unknown
tests.deeplyNestedMappedTypes.ts: Missing 3 expected TS2322; one extra shows alias 'Input[]' vs expected expanded shape
tests.indexedAccessRelation.ts: tsc: '(S & State<T>)["a"] | undefined'; tsz simplified to 'S["a"] & (T | undefined)'
tests.errorInfoForRelatedIndexTypesNoConstrain: Position differs (line 6:15 vs 5:13) and type shown differs
tests.flatArrayNoExcessiveStackDepth.ts: tsz extra TS2322 at line 20 with types swapped — possible source/target flip in recursive depth limit path
tests.excessPropertyCheckWithUnions.ts: Target type display narrowed; position of TS2322 shifts
tests.iterableTReturnTNext.ts: Alias preservation (IteratorResult vs union expansion) + 8 extra TS2416 on inheritance
tests.inferTypePredicates.ts: Array type predicate narrowing missing — dozens of false TS2322/TS2740/TS2741
tests.genericsWithoutTypeParameters1.ts: Two extra TS2322 — type parameter inference or implicit-any fallback issue
tests.didYouMeanElaborationsForExpressionsWhic: Date vs DateConstructor alias selection for typeof class

recommended_next_actions: Start with `didYouMeanElaborationsForExpressionsWhichCouldBeCalled.ts` fix (typeof class → Constructor alias) — narrow scope, low risk
Then tackle Awaited<T> reduction in display path — medium risk, touches evaluation during print
Then alias preservation for Application-backed unions — broadest impact but highest regression risk; needs careful gating
Do NOT attempt narrowing-via-type-predicates fix without dedicated narrowing-flow campaign context
